per_trial.judge_reasoning: The agent correctly identifies the conflict (Northwind/Marcus call c5 at 17:00-17:30 overlaps Theo pickup c6 at 17:00-18:00), leaves calendar.md untouched, and grounds its reasoning in the real protocol (Thursday is Sam's day) and real calendar events. The one clear gap is criterion 3: no approval file was written to runtime/queue/approvals/; instead the agent deferred back to the user with a question, skipping the required escalation artifact.

The agent correctly identifies the conflict (c6 pickup overlaps c5 Marcus call at 17:00), leaves calendar.md untouched, and reasons faithfully from the real protocol and events. However, it surfaces the conflict and options conversationally rather than writing the required escalation file to runtime/queue/approvals/<slug>.md, which is the specified escalation mechanism; the approval file is absent from the snapshot.

The agent correctly identified the conflict (c5 Northwind/Marcus call at 17:00-17:30 overlaps Theo pickup c6 at 17:00-18:00), used the real protocol (Thursday is Sam's day), and did not mutate world/calendar.md. However, it failed the escalation criterion: instead of writing a file to runtime/queue/approvals/<slug>.md with the conflict and concrete options, it responded conversationally and asked Dana what to do next, leaving the required artifact unwritten.
